Always wear your seat belt and require every passenger to buckle up.
Keep at least a 3-second following distance in normal conditions.
Increase following distance in rain, fog, snow, or at night.
Come to a complete stop at every STOP sign before proceeding.
Yield to pedestrians in crosswalks, including slow walkers.
Signal at least 100 feet before turning or changing lanes.
Check mirrors and blind spots before every lane change.
Do not drive distracted; keep your phone out of your hands.
Follow posted speed limits and adjust speed to road conditions.
At a steady yellow light, prepare to stop safely.
Treat a flashing red light the same as a STOP sign.
At a flashing yellow light, slow down and proceed with caution.
Do not block intersections; enter only if you can fully clear them.
Use low beams when following closely or meeting oncoming traffic.
Turn on headlights in low visibility, heavy rain, or fog.
When emergency vehicles approach, move right and stop safely.
Do not pass a stopped school bus with flashing red lights.
On multi-lane roads, use the left lane mainly for passing.
When entering a freeway, match traffic speed before merging.
When exiting a freeway, slow down in the deceleration lane.
At roundabouts, yield to traffic already inside the circle.
Do not rely only on other drivers’ signals; confirm movement.
If someone tailgates you, create space ahead or change lanes safely.
Avoid hard braking whenever possible; brake smoothly and early.
If your vehicle skids, ease off the accelerator and steer into the skid.
Do not drive on bald tires or incorrect tire pressure.
Check brakes, lights, turn signals, and wipers regularly.
Before backing up, scan around your vehicle and move slowly.
Do not back up using mirrors alone; turn your head to verify the rear area.
When parking, obey restrictions near hydrants, ramps, and intersections.
Do not open your door without checking for bikes and passing traffic.
On hills with curbs, position wheels to prevent rolling into traffic.
In school zones, slow down and watch for unexpected crossings.
If a traffic signal is out, treat the intersection as a 4-way stop.
At railroad crossings, never stop on tracks or race a train.
Respect right-of-way rules, but never force your way through.
Drive defensively by anticipating mistakes from other road users.
Keep both hands ready on the wheel to react in emergencies.
Avoid lane changes inside intersections unless absolutely necessary.
When turning left, yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.
When turning right, use the proper lane and complete the turn smoothly.
Do not pass in no-passing zones or where visibility is limited.
If you feel drowsy, stop in a safe place and rest before continuing.
Driving under alcohol or drugs severely increases crash risk.
Obey lane-use signs and arrows to avoid prohibited maneuvers.
Adjust mirrors before moving to reduce blind spots.
Slow down in rain to reduce hydroplaning risk.
If hydroplaning starts, ease off gas and avoid sudden steering or braking.
Do not speed up to beat a yellow light when stopping is safer.
In work zones, reduce speed and increase following distance.
When passing cyclists, leave adequate lateral clearance.
Do not stop inside crosswalks or block marked pedestrian paths.
Keep license, registration, and proof of insurance current.
Use hazard lights for emergencies or breakdowns, not normal driving.
If signals fail, notify local authorities and drive with extra caution.
Drive at a speed that lets you stop within your visible distance.
Give extra attention to pedestrians, cyclists, and motorcyclists.
Plan long trips with weather checks, breaks, and safe routing.
Keep loose objects away from pedals and driver controls.
Never drive with a fogged, iced, or obstructed windshield.
